name: ackl

metadata.name: ackl
metadata.summary: A Python library for kernels used in analytical chemistry

documentation:
# ackl

### Publication

    Analytical chemistry kernel library for spectroscopic profiling data, Food Chemistry Advances, Volume 3, 2023, 100342, ISSN 2772-753X, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.focha.2023.100342.

### PyPI (Python Package Index) repository

https://pypi.org/project/ackl/ 

### Reproducible Code-Ocean capsule

https://doi.org/10.24433/CO.4614220.v2


# Install (Ubuntu Env Setup)

```
!apt-get install r-base r-base-dev ffmpeg libsm6 libxext6 

!pip install rpy2
!pip install qsi==0.3.9
!pip install ackl==1.0.2
!pip install cla==1.1.4
!pip install opencv-python

# Post-install script

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

Rscript -e 'install.packages("ECoL")'
```

# Compile

```
python -m build --wheel  
python -m pyc_wheel x.whl  [optional]
```

# Use

### Kernel Response Patterns

```
import ackl.metrics
ackl.metrics.linear_response_pattern(20)
```

### Run Kernels on Target Dataset

```
_, dics, _ = ackl.metrics.classify_with_kernels(X, y,embed_title = False)
```

Show the result as HTML table and bar charts: 

```
html_str = ackl.metrics.visualize_metric_dicts(dics, plot = True)
display(HTML( html_str ))
```

functions.name: ackl.metrics.acc
documentation:
Use classification accuracy as a metric for kernel performance

Parameters
----------
X : expect an already-scaled data matrix
y : target labels. support bianary classification.
f : a kernelfunciton. Can use lambda to pass concrete kernel-specific parameters, e.g., 
    lambda x, y: laplacian_kernel(x, y, 2.0/N)
    lambda x, y: laplacian_kernel(x, y, 4.0/N)

functions.name: ackl.metrics.kes
documentation:
kernel effect size

Parameter
---------
f - a kernel function. Can use lambda to pass concrete kernel-specific parameters, e.g., 
    lambda x, y: laplacian_kernel(x, y, 2.0/N)
    lambda x, y: laplacian_kernel(x, y, 4.0/N)

Return
------
The max effect size of all the PLS components after kernel transform

functions.name: ackl.metrics.nmd
documentation:
Computes the normalized mean difference between the between-class and within-class matrices.

Parameter
---------
f - a kernel function. Can use lambda to pass concrete kernel-specific parameters, e.g., 
    lambda x, y: laplacian_kernel(x, y, 2.0/N)
    lambda x, y: laplacian_kernel(x, y, 4.0/N)

Return
------
ratio

functions.name: ackl.metrics.binary_response_pattern
documentation:
Generates the simplest response pattern for just two points 0 and 1

       0      1 
0  k(0,0) k(0,1)
1  k(1,0) k(1,1)

Parameter
---------
cmap : color map scheme

functions.name: ackl.metrics.linear_response_pattern
documentation:
Generates a response pattern of pairwise-point-distances.
See how each kernel responds to / varies with linearly arranged points.

Parameter
---------
n : sample size. 
dim : how many dimensions to use for the sample data
    If dim = 1, will use n natural series, i.e., 1,2,3,4... 
    If dim = 2, will use two 2d series, i.e., (0,1),(0,2),(0,3),(0,4)... and (1,0),(2,0),(3,0),(4,0)...
cmap : color map scheme

functions.name: ackl.metrics.classify_with_kernels
documentation:
Try various kernel types for classification task.  
Each kernel uses their own default/empirical paramters.    
In binary classification, because the first and last half samples belong to two classes respectively. 
We want to get a contrasive pattern, i.e., (LT、RB) and （LB、RT）have blocks of different colors. 

Parameters
----------
X : an m-by-n data matrix. 
    Should be already rescaled to non-negative ranges (required by chi2 family) 
    and re-ordered by y. 
y : class labels
cmap : color map scheme to use. set None to use default, or set another scheme, e.g., 'gray', 'viridis', 'jet', 'rainbow', etc.
    For small dataset, we recommend 'gray'. For complex dataset, we recommend 'viridis'.
hyper_param_optimizer : which optimizer to optimize the hyper parameters for each kernel. 
    For real-world dataset, use kes by default. For toy dataset, set None to disable optimizer.
    For multi-class dataset (y has more than 5 classes), use acc.
scale : whether do feature scaling
clfs : a list of multi-class classfiers. pass 'all' to use all available classifiers.
multi_kernels : how many kernels to use for MKL combination. Default is [1].
    If a string, e.g., "poly+gaussian+cosine+spline" or "poly,gaussian,cosine,spline", will use this specific MKL combination.
multi_kernel_topk : the k best single kernels to use in multi-kernel combinations. Default is 5.
logplot : whether to output the log-scale plot in parallel.
plots : whether to ouptut the kernel heatmaps and the scatter plots after PCA / PLS, to check classifiability.
    The PLS tries to maximize the covariance between X and Y.
embed_title : whether embed the title in the plots. If not, will generate the title in HTML.
do_cla : whether to do classifiability analysis using the cla package.
selected_kernel_names : a list of kernel names to be process. 
    If None or 'all', will use all kernels.

functions.name: ackl.metrics.visualize_acc_dict
documentation:
Visualize the acc result dictionary returned by classify_with_kernels()

Return
------
The best top-1 acc

Example
-------
_, test_acc_dic, _ = classify_with_kernels(X, y, ...)
visualize_metric_dicts(test_acc_dic) # use in jupyter notebook

functions.name: ackl.metrics.visualize_cla_dicts
documentation:
Example
-------
_, _, dics, _ = classify_with_kernels(X, y, cla = True)
visualize_metric_dicts(dicts) # use in jupyter notebook

functions.name: ackl.metrics.optimize_kernel_hparam
documentation:
Paramters
---------
X : need to re-order X by y first

functions.name: ackl.metrics.cosine_kernel_response_pattern
documentation:
Special demo for the cosine kernel with 2D dataset. 
We use equal-angle-interval input. While for other kernels, we use equal-interval input.

functions.name: ackl.metrics.time_cost_kernels
documentation:
Time cost of kernels

return
------
dct_mu : a dict of mean time cost of each kernel

functions.name: ackl.metrics.plot_components_2d
documentation: Copied from qsi.vis.plot_components_2d, to avoid package dependency.

modules.name: ackl.kernels
documentation: This file contains definitions for all kernels.

functions.name: ackl.kernels.gaussian_kernel
documentation: Gaussian kernel. An alias of sklearn.metrics.pairwise.rbf_kernel

functions.name: ackl.kernels.exponential_kernel
documentation: We use exponental and laplacian kernels interchangably.

functions.name: ackl.kernels.rq_kernel
documentation:
The Rational Quadratic kernel. Using sklearn.gaussian_process.kernels.RationalQuadratic.
k(x_i, x_j) = \left(1 + \frac{d(x_i, x_j)^2 }{ 2\alpha  l^2}\right)^{-\alpha}

Paramters
---------
a - scale mixture
l - length scale

functions.name: ackl.kernels.spline_kernel
documentation:
The spline kernel. We provide two flavors of implementation.

Parameters
----------
k : preprocessed dims. 
    The spline kernel doesnot work well on high-dimensional spectroscopic data, 
    so we can perform a pre-processing DimRed.  
flavor : 1 - self implmentation
    2 - use the Spline() class

functions.name: ackl.kernels.expmin_kernel
documentation:
exponential min kernel.
K(x,y) = exp(-a min (|x-y|,|x+y|))^2

functions.name: ackl.kernels.ghi_kernel
documentation: Generalized Histogram Intersection kernel

functions.name: ackl.kernels.wavelet_kernel
documentation:
The wavelet is a family / series. This is just a commonly used specific implementation.
Use "import pywt, pywt.wavelist()" to check a full list of wavelets.

functions.name: ackl.kernels.log_kernel
documentation: On real data, we found the log kernel is insensative to param d. In most cases, use d's default value.

functions.name: ackl.kernels.matern_kernel
documentation:
The matern kernel. 
Implemented according to the math definition.

Parameter
---------
v : controls smoothness. when v = 1/2, it becomes into the laplacian/exp kernel. 
s : controls scaling

functions.name: ackl.kernels.ess_kernel
documentation:
Parameter
---------
p : periodical parameter
s : scale parameter

functions.name: ackl.kernels.fejer_kernel
documentation:
Parameter
---------
k - order of fejer series. Usually we don't use k = 1 as it always equals 1.

functions.name: ackl.kernels.wave_kernel
documentation:
The wave kernel is actually sinc. 
When s = pi(3.14), it is normalized sinc.

functions.name: ackl.kernels.cosine_kernel
documentation:
This is a self-implemented version, which uses a different 
normalization from sklearn.metrics.pairwise.cosine_similarity().

functions.name: ackl.kernels.euclidean_dist_matrix
documentation: Returns matrix of pairwise, squared Euclidean distances

functions.name: ackl.kernels.default_wavelet
documentation: Default wavelet is the morlet wavelet.

functions.name: ackl.kernels.graphs_to_adjacency_lists
documentation:
Given a list of graphs, output a numpy.array
containing their adjacency matices.

functions.name: ackl.kernels.relabel
documentation:
Given list of labels for each graph in the dataset,
rename them so they belong to set {1, ..., num_labels},
where num_labels is number of the distinct labels.
Return tuple consisting of new labels and maximal label.

functions.name: ackl.kernels.floyd_warshall
documentation: Returns matrix of shortest path weights.

functions.name: ackl.kernels._apply_floyd_warshall
documentation:
Applies Floyd-Warshall algorithm on a dataset.
Returns a tuple containing dataset of FW transformates and max path length

variables.name: ackl.kernels.kernel_names
documentation:
The following codes are based on https://github.com/gmum/pykernels. (MIT Licence)
original authors: Wojciech Marian Czarnecki and Katarzyna Janocha

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Kernel

methods.name: ackl.kernels.Kernel._compute
documentation:
Main method which given two lists data_1 and data_2, with
N and M elements respectively should return a kernel matrix
of size N x M where K_{ij} = K(data_1_i, data_2_j)

methods.name: ackl.kernels.Kernel.gram
documentation: Returns a Gramian, kernel matrix of matrix and itself

methods.name: ackl.kernels.Kernel.dim
documentation: Returns dimension of the feature space
Base, abstract kernel class

classes.name: ackl.kernels.KernelSum
documentation: Represents sum of a pair of kernels

classes.name: ackl.kernels.KernelProduct
documentation: Represents product of a pair of kernels

classes.name: ackl.kernels.KernelPower
documentation: Represents natural power of a kernel

classes.name: ackl.kernels.ScaledKernel
documentation: Represents kernel scaled by a float

classes.name: ackl.kernels.GraphKernel
documentation: Base, abstract GraphKernel kernel class

classes.name: ackl.kernels.RationalQuadratic2
documentation:
Rational quadratic kernel, V2. This implementation is differnt from sklearn. By default, we use sklearn.

    k(x, y) = 1 - ||x-y||^2/(||x-y||^2+c)

where:
    c > 0

classes.name: ackl.kernels.InverseMultiquadratic
documentation:
Inverse multiquadratic kernel, 

    k(x, y) = 1 / sqrt(||x-y||^2 + c^2)

where:
    c > 0

as defined in:
"Interpolation of scattered data: Distance matrices and conditionally positive definite functions"
Charles Micchelli
Constructive Approximation

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Cauchy
documentation:
Cauchy kernel, 

    k(x, y) = 1 / (1 + ||x - y||^2 / s ^ 2)

where:
    s = sigma

as defined in:
"A least square kernel machine with box constraints"
Jayanta Basak
International Conference on Pattern Recognition 2008

classes.name: ackl.kernels.TStudent
documentation:
T-Student kernel, 

    k(x, y) = 1 / (1 + ||x - y||^d)

where:
    d = degree

as defined in:
"Alternative Kernels for Image Recognition"
Sabri Boughorbel, Jean-Philippe Tarel, Nozha Boujemaa
INRIA - INRIA Activity Reports - RalyX
http://ralyx.inria.fr/2004/Raweb/imedia/uid84.html

classes.name: ackl.kernels.ANOVA
documentation:
ANOVA kernel, 
    k(x, y) = SUM_k exp( -sigma * (x_k - y_k)^2 )^d

as defined in

"Kernel methods in machine learning"
Thomas Hofmann, Bernhard Scholkopf and Alexander J. Smola
The Annals of Statistics
http://www.kernel-machines.org/publications/pdfs/0701907.pdf

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Wavelet
documentation:
Wavelet kernel,

    k(x, y) = PROD_i h( (x_i-c)/a ) h( (y_i-c)/a )

or for c = None

    k(x, y) = PROD_i h( (x_i - y_i)/a )

as defined in
"Wavelet Support Vector Machine"
Li Zhang, Weida Zhou, Licheng Jiao
IEEE Transactions on System, Man, and Cybernetics

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Fourier
documentation:
Fourier kernel,

    k(x, y) = PROD_i (1-q^2)/(2(1-2q cos(x_i-y_i)+q^2))

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Tanimoto
documentation:
Tanimoto kernel
    k(x, y) = <x, y> / (||x||^2 + ||y||^2 - <x, y>)

as defined in:

"Graph Kernels for Chemical Informatics"
Liva Ralaivola, Sanjay J. Swamidass, Hiroto Saigo and Pierre Baldi
Neural Networks
http://citeseerx.ist.psu.edu/viewdoc/download?doi=10.1.1.92.483&rep=rep1&type=pdf

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Sorensen
documentation:
Sorensen kernel
    k(x, y) = 2 <x, y> / (||x||^2 + ||y||^2)

as defined in:

"Graph Kernels for Chemical Informatics"
Liva Ralaivola, Sanjay J. Swamidass, Hiroto Saigo and Pierre Baldi
Neural Networks
http://citeseerx.ist.psu.edu/viewdoc/download?doi=10.1.1.92.483&rep=rep1&type=pdf

classes.name: ackl.kernels.PositiveKernel
documentation: Defines kernels which can be only used with positive values

classes.name: ackl.kernels.AdditiveChi2
documentation:
Additive Chi^2 kernel, 
    k(x, y) = SUM_i 2 x_i y_i / (x_i + y_i)

as defined in

"Efficient Additive Kernels via Explicit Feature Maps"
Andrea Vedaldi, Andrew Zisserman
IEEE TRANSACTIONS ON PATTERN ANALYSIS AND MACHINE INTELLIGENCE
http://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~vedaldi/assets/pubs/vedaldi11efficient.pdf

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Chi2
documentation:
Chi^2 kernel, 
    k(x, y) = exp( -gamma * SUM_i (x_i - y_i)^2 / (x_i + y_i) )

as defined in:

"Local features and kernels for classification 
 of texture and object categories: A comprehensive study"
Zhang, J. and Marszalek, M. and Lazebnik, S. and Schmid, C. 
International Journal of Computer Vision 2007 
http://eprints.pascal-network.org/archive/00002309/01/Zhang06-IJCV.pdf

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Min
documentation:
Min kernel (also known as Histogram intersection kernel)
    k(x, y) = SUM_i min(x_i, y_i)

classes.name: ackl.kernels.GeneralizedHistogramIntersection
documentation:
Generalized histogram intersection kernel
    k(x, y) = SUM_i min(|x_i|^alpha, |y_i|^alpha)

as defined in
"Generalized histogram intersection kernel for image recognition"
Sabri Boughorbel, Jean-Philippe Tarel, Nozha Boujemaa
International Conference on Image Processing (ICIP-2005)
http://perso.lcpc.fr/tarel.jean-philippe/publis/jpt-icip05.pdf

classes.name: ackl.kernels.MinMax
documentation:
MinMax kernel
    k(x, y) = SUM_i min(x_i, y_i) / SUM_i max(x_i, y_i)

bounded by [0,1] as defined in:

"Graph Kernels for Chemical Informatics"
Liva Ralaivola, Sanjay J. Swamidass, Hiroto Saigo and Pierre Baldi
Neural Networks
http://citeseerx.ist.psu.edu/viewdoc/download?doi=10.1.1.92.483&rep=rep1&type=pdf

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Spline
documentation:
Spline kernel, 
    k(x, y) = PROD_i 1 + x_iy_i + x_iy_i min(x_i,y_i)
                       - (x_i+y_i)/2 * min(x_i,y_i)^2
                       + 1/3 * min(x_i, y_i)^3

as defined in

"Support Vector Machines for Classification and Regression"
Steve Gunn
ISIS Technical Report
http://www.svms.org/tutorials/Gunn1998.pdf

classes.name: ackl.kernels.ConditionalyPositiveDefiniteKernel
documentation: Defines kernels which are only CPD

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Log
documentation:
Log kernel
    k(x, y) = -log(||x-y||^d + 1)

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Power
documentation:
Power kernel
    k(x, y) = -||x-y||^d

as defined in:
"Scale-Invariance of Support Vector Machines based on the Triangular Kernel"
Hichem Sahbi, Francois Fleuret
Research report
https://hal.inria.fr/inria-00071984

classes.name: ackl.kernels.Graph
documentation:
Basic Graph class.
Can be labeled by edges or nodes.

classes.name: ackl.kernels.ShortestPath

methods.name: ackl.kernels.ShortestPath._create_accum_list_labeled
documentation:
Construct accumulation array matrix for one dataset
containing labaled graph data.

methods.name: ackl.kernels.ShortestPath._create_accum_list
documentation:
Construct accumulation array matrix for one dataset
containing unlabaled graph data.
Shortest Path kernel [3]
